column1: # Per-case deliberate divergences, keyed to the reconciliation verdicts
# (DESIGN Decision 2). A row here tells the harness to expect a specific
# difference between the engine's fired set and the external set for one case,
# so the parity test asserts the chosen behavior instead of failing blindly.
#   case_id    the case this divergence applies to
#   token      an engine code (direction=engine_only) or an external rule
#   direction  engine_only   -- the engine fires `token` where the external
#                               check has no matching rule (the absorbed check
#              external_only -- the external check fires `token` but the engine
#                               deliberately does not (an edge left unabsorbed).
#   reason     one-line rationale, traceable to a Decision 2 verdict
# The proving frontmatter cases are at parity, so there are no rows yet. A real
# reconciliation (e.g. FC05's section-order superset) adds an engine_only row.
